File.Name: m823b_m823-013.jpg
Notes: 52306688706 This is bumble bee-like wild bee on new flower of native California Aster (Corethrogyne filaginifolia, Asteraceae) in the woods today. I guess it is a male Long-horned Bee in the genus Melissodes or Eucera with those long antennae. The males do have hairy legs, but the females have even hairier legs with proper pollen baskets (scopae) designed to carry pollen. (San Marcos Pass, 23 August 2022)

File.Name: m823c_m820-007.jpg
Notes: 52305959477 Here are another mating pair of Stink Bugs in the family Pentatomidae with 5-segmented antennae on native Bird's Beak (Cordylanthus rigidus, Orobanchaceae) in the woods today. I find this a confusing group, but this one might be the Western Red-shouldered Stink Bug (Thyanta pallidovirens), though there are other possibilities. It's almost Wednesday, so Happy Hump Day! (San Marcos Pass, 20 August 2022)
CommonNames: Bird's Beak; Western Red-shouldered Stink Bug

File.Name: m824a_m824-013.jpg
Notes: 52309473298 Here is a handsome Lorquin's Admiral (Basilarchia lorquini aka Limenitis lorquini) butterfly perched on a leaf of native Arroyo Willow (Salix lasiolepis, Salicaceae) down by the creek today. Compare the similar California Sister (Adelpha bredowii) butterfly in this photo from yesterday. (San Marcos Pass, 24 August 2022)

File.Name: m824b_m822-037.jpg
Notes: 52308264117 This is a large spider web in the woods in the woods a few days ago. It's a couple of feet across -- not huge, but the rigging lines span a good twenty feet which helps create a sense of size. This is one of those webs than can span a road that you don't want to walk into at night -- where's the spider?! I'm sure the spider is genus Araneus in the family of Orb Web Weavers (Araneidae). Happy Web Webnesday! (San Marcos Pass, 22 August 2022)

File.Name: m824c_m820-032.jpg
Notes: 52309430048 Here is a tiny Six-spotted Orb Weaver (Araniella displicata, Araneidae) on its web stretched across of leaf of Coast Live Oak (Quercus agrifolia, Fagaceae) in the woods a few days ago. These tiny spiders are only about 1/8 inch long or less. Note that this one has eight spots, four on each side of its abdomen. Despite its common name, the spiders in our canyon (at least) all seem to have eight spots rather than six. Happy Web Webnesday! (San Marcos Pass, 20 August 2022)
CommonNames: Six-spotted Orb Weaver; Coast Live Oak

File.Name: m825a_m825-014.jpg
Notes: 52311548849 This is another California Sister (Adelpha bredowii) butterfly on leaves of native Arroyo Willow (Salix lasiolepis, Salicaceae) down by the creek today. Compare the similar Lorquin's Admiral (Limenitis lorquini) butterfly in this photo from yesterday. (San Marcos Pass, 25 August 2022)

File.Name: m825b_m825-002.jpg
Notes: 52311555420 This is a "just a fly" (Diptera) on a leaf of Coast Live Oak (Quercus agrifolia, Fagaceae) in the woods today. I don't know what kind of fly it is, but it looks bristly enough to be another Tachinid Fly in the family Tachinidae. HBBBT! (San Marcos Pass, 25 August 2022)

File.Name: m825c_m825-027.jpg
Notes: 52310295787 Here is a Western Paper Wasp (Mischocyttarus flavitarsis) getting a drink on the water tub by the dry creek today. This is the wasp that has its abdomen on a stalk or petiole. HBBBT! (San Marcos Pass, 25 August 2022)

File.Name: m826a_m826-028.jpg
Notes: 52312465102 Here is a tough-looking Pacific Ambush Bug (Phymata pacifica) on a new flower of the summer-flowering native shrub Coyote Bush (Baccharis pilularis, Asteraceae) in the woods today -- the first flowers I've seen this year. Yes, these predators are as effective as they look, see this photo for an example. (San Marcos Pass, 26 August 2022)

File.Name: m826b_m824-005.jpg
Notes: 52313249786 This is another dark little Bee Fly (Bombyliidae) on an new flower of native California Aster (Corethrogyne filaginifolia, Asteraceae) in the woods a few days ago. I believe the fly is the Sinuous Bee Fly (Hemipenthes sinuosa) or kin in the Villini tribe. It looks quite black in the wild, but it looks quite brown in my photos. HFDF! (San Marcos Pass, 24 August 2022)

File.Name: m826c_m821-013.jpg
Notes: 52312446342 This is a Stiletto Fly in the family Therevidae on a leaf of Coast Live Oak (Quercus agrifolia, Fagaceae) tree in the woods a few days ago, see this photo . Maybe it's the species Thereva fucata, see this BugGuide info page. I've seen these handsome flies before, but not often. HFDF! (San Marcos Pass, 21 August 2022)

File.Name: m827a_m826-019.jpg
Notes: 52315723088 I found this rattlesnake on our road yesterday in the middle of a nice meal that appears to be a large rat. The rat's tail is sticking out of the snake's mouth! I guess that it's a Southern Pacific Rattlesnake (Crotalus oreganus helleri and it's prey is a Dusky-Footed Wood Rat (Neotoma fuscipes) with a furry tail. I left it alone and when I returned it was gone. (San Marcos Pass, 26 August 2022)
CommonNames: Southern Pacific Rattlesnake; Dusky-Footed Wood Rat

File.Name: m827b_m826-006.jpg
Notes: 52315823204 This is a nymph of a Leafhopper Assassin Bug (Zelus renardii, Reduviidae) on a dusty leaf of native Coast Live Oak (Quercus agrifolia, Fagaceae) in the woods yesterday. (San Marcos Pass, 26 August 2022)
CommonNames: Leafhopper Assassin Bug; Coast Live Oak

File.Name: m827c_m823-016.jpg
Notes: 52315815009 This is wild bee on new flower of native California Aster (Corethrogyne filaginifolia, Asteraceae) in the woods a few days ago, see this photo. I guess it is a male Long-horned Bee in the genus Melissodes or Eucera with those long antennae. The males do have hairy legs, but the females have even hairier legs with proper pollen baskets (scopae) designed to carry pollen. (San Marcos Pass, 23 August 2022)

File.Name: m828a_m828-020.jpg
Notes: 52318339249 This is a honeybee-size Bee Fly (order Bombyliidae, Diptera) on the ground in the woods today. Maybe it's genus Villa, and perhaps Villa agrippina. (San Marcos Pass, 28 August 2022)

File.Name: m828b_m828-004.jpg
Notes: 52317110052 This is another cute little net-winged Planthopper (Fulgoroidea) on a branch of Coast Live Oak (Quercus agrifolia, Fagaceae) in the woods today. I believe it is genus Neaethus in the family Tropiduchidae. (San Marcos Pass, 28 August 2022)

File.Name: m828c_m828-002.jpg
Notes: 52318341390 This is tiny Orb-Weaver Spider (Araneus, Araneidae) spider in a leaf Coast Live Oak (Quercus agrifolia, Fagaceae) in the woods yesterday. I believe this one is either Araneus bispinosus or Araneus montereyensis. (San Marcos Pass, 28 August 2022)

File.Name: m829a_m829-043.jpg
Notes: 52321105384 Here is a Common Buckeye (Junonia coenia) butterfly in the woods today. They can sit quietly for a long time with their wings closed, looking quite plain, and then open their wings in a quick flash! (San Marcos Pass, 29 August 2022)

File.Name: m829b_m829-014.jpg
Notes: 52320650156 I found this impressive Long-horned Orthopteran (Ensifera, Orthoptera) in a old faded flower of Southern Bush Monkeyflower (Diplacus longiflorus, Phrymaceae) in the woods today. It's probably a couple of inches long. It looks like a katydid with those long antennae, but I'm confused that its gray rather than green. It might be a short-winged Uniform Shieldback (Idiostatus aequalis), compare this this photo from BugGuide. (San Marcos Pass, 29 August 2022)

File.Name: m829c_m829-037.jpg
Notes: 52321062214 Here is a tough-looking Pacific Ambush Bug (Phymata pacifica) on a new flower of the summer-flowering native shrub of Sawtooth Goldenbush (Hazardia squarrosa, Asteraceae) in the woods today. (San Marcos Pass, 29 August 2022)

File.Name: m830a_m830-011.jpg
Notes: 52323499189 This are the tiny nymphs of true bugs (Heteroptera, Hemiptera) on a leaf of Bitter Gooseberry (Ribes amarum, Grossulariaceae) in the woods today. I''m not sure what kind of bugs they are, but compare this photo with tiny hatchlings. (San Marcos Pass, 30 August 2022)

File.Name: m830b_m830-013.jpg
Notes: 52323497600 I found this adult Katydid (Tettigoniidae) in an leaf of a Coast Live Oak (Quercus agrifolia, Fagaceae) in the woods today. I believe this is one of the Bush Katydids in the genus Scudderia - probably the Mexican Bush Katydid (Scudderia mexicana). We also have Angle-winged Katydids in the genus Microcentrum with plain green nymphs and more leaf-like wings, see this photo. The adults of both kinds seem to be much less common than the nymphs. (San Marcos Pass, 30 August 2022)
CommonNames: Coast Live Oak; Mexican Bush Katydid

File.Name: m830c_m830-051.jpg
Notes: 52322227387 Here are Yellowjacket Wasps (Vespula) getting a drink on the water tub by our dry creek today. (San Marcos Pass, 30 August 2022)

File.Name: m831a_m831-015.jpg
Notes: 52325888149 Here are mating Pacific Ambush Bugs (Phymata pacifica) on a new flower of the summer-flowering native shrub Coyote Bush (Baccharis pilularis, Asteraceae) in the woods today. Happy Hump Day! (San Marcos Pass, 31 August 2022)

File.Name: m831b_m826-013.jpg
Notes: 52325760178 This is Orb-Weaver Spider (Araneus, Araneidae) spider web in small Coast Live Oak (Quercus agrifolia, Fagaceae) tree in the woods a few days ago. You can see the tiny spider on side, either Araneus bispinosus or Araneus montereyensis, see this photo. Happy Web Webnesday! (San Marcos Pass, 26 August 2022)

File.Name: m831c_m826-016.jpg
Notes: 52325865029 This is spider webbing on native Deerweed (Acmispon glaber, Fabaceae) in the woods a few days ago. Happy Web Webnesday! (San Marcos Pass, 26 August 2022)

File.Name: m901a_m901-016.jpg
Notes: 52328326990 This is a remarkable wasp (Hymenoptera) visiting new flowers of native shrub Coyote Bush (Baccharis pilularis, Asteraceae) in the woods today. I believe it's a Carrot Wasp in the family Gasteruptiidae, almost certainly genus Gasteruption. (San Marcos Pass, 1 September 2022)

File.Name: m901b_m831-008.jpg
Notes: 52328126863 This is a male Tree Cricket (genus Oecanthus, Gryllidae) on a dead leaf of Coast Live Oak (Quercus agrifolia, Fagaceae) tree in the woods yesterday. The females have narrow wings, while the males have broad wings that they rub together to make their song -- see this photo where I caught one in the act. Only the males sing. HBBBT! (San Marcos Pass, 31 August 2022)

File.Name: m901c_m830-008.jpg
Notes: 52326919782 This are more tiny nymphs of true bugs (Heteroptera, Hemiptera) on a leaf of Bitter Gooseberry (Ribes amarum, Grossulariaceae) in the woods a few days ago. I''m not sure what kind of bugs they are, but compare this photo with tiny hatchlings. HBBBT! (San Marcos Pass, 30 August 2022)

File.Name: m902a_m902-002.jpg
Notes: 52330519590 I believe this is an atypical Bee Fly in the family Bombyliidae, probably in the genus Geron. I found it on new flower of native Plummer's Baccharis aka Smooth Baccharis (Baccharis plummerae, Asteraceae) which is just starting to flower. HFDF! (San Marcos Pass, 2 September 2022)
CommonNames: Bee Fly; Plummer's Baccharis; Smooth Baccharis

File.Name: m902b_m829-006.jpg
Notes: 52329127357 This is a "just a fly" (Diptera) on a leaf of Southern Bush Monkeyflower (Diplacus longiflorus, Phrymaceae) in the woods a few days ago. I don't know what kind of fly it is, but it looks bristly enough to be another Tachinid Fly in the family Tachinidae. HFDF! (San Marcos Pass, 29 August 2022)

File.Name: m902c_m827-025.jpg
Notes: 52330068466 This is a dancing fly (Diptera) in the woods a few days ago. I already have my two photos for the Fly Day Friday group, but HFDF anyway! (San Marcos Pass, 27 August 2022)

File.Name: m903a_m903-022.jpg
Notes: 52332637335 This is a Bembecine Sand Wasp (genus Steniolia, Bembicini, Hymenoptera) on new flowers of native California Aster (Corethrogyne filaginifolia, Asteraceae) in the woods today. Yep, they have remarkable goggle-like eyes, but what gives with that amazing "third eye" between its two real eyes -- the mark of the Illuminati?? (San Marcos Pass, 3 September 2022)

File.Name: m903b_m903-014.jpg
Notes: 52332436448 I found this adult Katydid (Tettigoniidae) in flowering shrub of Coyote Bush (Baccharis pilularis, Asteraceae) in the woods today. I believe this is one of the Bush Katydids in the genus Scudderia -- probably the Mexican Bush Katydid (Scudderia mexicana). (San Marcos Pass, 3 September 2022)

File.Name: m903c_m903-011.jpg
Notes: 52332489349 I'm sure this is an adult Oedaleonotus phryneicus (Melanoplinae) grasshopper, and those little stubs are all the wings it will ever get. This one is perched in a shrub of Southern Bush Monkeyflower (Mimulus aurantiacus var. pubescens, Phrymaceae). (San Marcos Pass, 3 September 2022)

File.Name: m904a_m904-020.jpg
Notes: 52334734451 This is wild bee on new flowers of native California Aster (Corethrogyne filaginifolia, Asteraceae) in the woods today. I believe this bee is family Megachilidae. These bees collect pollen under their abdomens rather than on their hind legs like most other bees, see the photo on the left. (San Marcos Pass, 4 September 2022)

File.Name: m904b_m904-012.jpg
Notes: 52333761782 This is wild bee on new flower of native Sawtooth Goldenbush (Hazardia squarrosa, Asteraceae) in the woods today. I guess it is a male Long-horned Bee in the genus Melissodes or Eucera with those long antennae. The males do have hairy legs, but the females have even hairier legs with proper pollen baskets (scopae) designed to carry pollen. (San Marcos Pass, 4 September 2022)

File.Name: m904c_m902-037.jpg
Notes: 52334940383 I found this impressive wasp (Hymenoptera) on in the water tub we keep by the summer-dry creek. I believe it's an Ichneumon Wasp in the family Ichneumonidae -- superfamily Ichneumonoidea for sure. These are parasitic wasps that lay their eggs on the larvae of other insects, see this photo for an example. That's pretty gruesome, but it's essential to preserving any kind of balance in nature. (San Marcos Pass, 2 September 2022)

File.Name: m905a_m905-019.jpg
Notes: 52337512638 This is another Woodland Skipper (Ochlodes sylvanoides) butterfly of native Sawtooth Goldenbush (Hazardia squarrosa, Asteraceae) in the woods today. These butterflies are distinctive mostly by having nothing very distinctive about them -- plain but handsome. (San Marcos Pass, 5 September 2022)

File.Name: m905b_m905-009.jpg
Notes: 52337559739 This is another little "looper caterpillar" of a Geometrid Moth (Geometridae, Lepidoptera) on a stem of native Golden Yarrow (Eriophyllum confertflorum, Asteraceae) in the woods today. You can see that it's standing on two pairs of hind prolegs on the left with three pairs of true legs on the other end. (San Marcos Pass, 5 September 2022)

File.Name: m905c_m905-024.jpg
Notes: 52337233161 This is another wild bee on new flower of native California Aster (Corethrogyne filaginifolia, Asteraceae) in the woods today. I now believe this bee is genus Ashmeadiella. These bees collect pollen under their abdomens rather than on their hind legs like most other bees, see this photo from yesterday. (San Marcos Pass, 5 September 2022)

File.Name: m906a_m906-010.jpg
Notes: 52339856448 This is a nymph of a Leafhopper Assassin Bug (Zelus renardii, Reduviidae) on a leaf of native Coast Live Oak (Quercus agrifolia, Fagaceae) in the woods today. (San Marcos Pass, 6 September 2022)
CommonNames: Leafhopper Assassin Bug; Coast Live Oak

File.Name: m906b_m905-040.jpg
Notes: 52339602671 I'm pretty sure this is a small Cuckoo Wasp (Family Chrysididae) in the order Hymenoptera, maybe in the genus Chrysis, in the woods yesterday. I believe their larvae are raised in the nests of other wasps and bees, hence the common name. (San Marcos Pass, 5 September 2022)

File.Name: m906c_m901-029.jpg
Notes: 52340012465 I believe these are mating Potter Wasps (Eumeninae, Vespidea) in the water tub we keep by the summer-dry creek in the woods few days ago. They are in a mating position, but there is no actual contact between them. It's almost Wednesday, so Happy Hump Day! (San Marcos Pass, 1 September 2022)

File.Name: m907a_m907-010.jpg
Notes: 52340881487 These are two different large insects in the water tub we keep by the summer-dry creek in the woods today. On the left is a large Black Soldier Fly (Hermetia illucens, Stratiomyidae). It's maybe 3/4 inch long. These flies have antennae like an old-fashioned TV set. I believe the wasp on the right is a Steel-blue Cricket-hunter Wasp (Chlorion aerarium). (San Marcos Pass, 7 September 2022)
CommonNames: Black Soldier Fly; Steel-blue Cricket-hunter Wasp

File.Name: m907b_m906-011.jpg
Notes: 52342261275 This is an Orb-Weaver Spider (Araneus, Araneidae) spider web in small Coast Live Oak (Quercus agrifolia, Fagaceae) tree in the woods yesterday. Happy Web Webnesday! (San Marcos Pass, 6 September 2022)

File.Name: m907c_m904-005.jpg
Notes: 52342121559 This is the web of a fairly large Funnel Web Weaver (Agelenidae) spider that's being turned up by the wind. Happy Web Webnesday! (San Marcos Pass, 4 September 2022)

File.Name: m908a_m908-008.jpg
Notes: 52344101001 I was surprised to find this Gray Hairstreak (Strymon melinus) butterfly working the flowers of Coyote Bush (Baccharis pilularis, Asteraceae) in the woods today. It is the first one I've seen this year. (San Marcos Pass, 8 September 2022)

File.Name: m908b_m908-029.jpg
Notes: 52344523610 Here is a Yellowjacket Wasp (Vespula) getting a drink on the water tub by our dry creek today. HBBBT! (San Marcos Pass, 8 September 2022)

File.Name: m908c_m905-035.jpg
Notes: 52343128647 Here is an attractive little fly (Diptera) on the water tub we keep by the summer-dry creek in the woods a few days ago. It might be species Minettia flaveola in the family Lauxaniidae -- compare this photo at BugGuide. HBBBT! (San Marcos Pass, 5 September 2022)

File.Name: m909a_m909-002.jpg
Notes: 52346707810 I found this beautiful beetle in the bathroom last night. I believe it's a Green Bark Beetle (Temnoscheila), probably species Temnoscheila chlorodia. The fantastic color is iridescent, flashing between green and blue, see this photo. They are said to be predaceous, foraging under the bark of dead trees. But what was it doing in our bathroom?? (San Marcos Pass, 9 September 2022)

File.Name: m909b_m909-029.jpg
Notes: 52346581029 This is a "just a fly" (Diptera) getting a drink on the water tub by our dry creek today. I don't know what kind of fly it is, but it looks bristly enough to be another Tachinid Fly in the family Tachinidae. HFDF! (San Marcos Pass, 9 September 2022)

File.Name: m909c_m908-028.jpg
Notes: 52346570324 This is a large Black Soldier Fly (Hermetia illucens, Stratiomyidae) getting a drink on the water tub by our dry creek yesterday. It's maybe 3/4 inch long. These flies have antennae like an old-fashioned TV set and wonderful patterned eyes. HFDF! (San Marcos Pass, 8 September 2022)

File.Name: m910a_m910-022.jpg
Notes: 52348746853 This a tattered Common Buckeye (Junonia coenia) butterfly on the chipseal road in our canyon today. (San Marcos Pass, 10 September 2022)
